chunk_summary: Guidelines for integrating macronutrient counseling into clinic workflows, linking children to nutrition programs, and addressing common myths with practical scripts.
learning_objectives: Understand how to document and classify growth and nutrition status in children using WHO z-scores or IAP BMI status.
Learn to link children with undernutrition to appropriate programs like ICDS/Mission POSHAN 2.0 and PM POSHAN.
Develop skills to address common nutrition myths using evidence-based counseling scripts.
Recognize when to refer children with severe malnutrition to facility-based management programs.
key_facts: A usable nutrition note includes growth classification, nutrition diagnosis, specific food changes, and a dated follow-up plan.
PM POSHAN meals provide 450 kcal and 12 g protein for primary and 700 kcal and 20 g protein for upper primary children.
Children under 6 years with undernutrition should be linked to anganwadi services under ICDS/Mission POSHAN 2.0.
Severe wasting, edema, or complications require referral to a nutrition rehabilitation center under NHM.
Common myths include: 'Juice is not fruit; give whole fruit and water' and 'Protein powders are not needed; dal, milk, egg, or fish is safer and cheaper.'
reference_quote: Children under 6 years with undernutrition or food insecurity should be linked to anganwadi services under ICDS/Mission POSHAN 2.0 for supplementary nutrition and counseling.
document_key_topics: Linking children to nutrition programs like ICDS and PM POSHAN
Addressing common nutrition myths with practical scripts
Referral and management of severe malnutrition cases
